hanafi.qiyas_basis: Creature causes immediate physical harm via venomous sting.
hanafi.exceptions: None. Permissible to kill at all times, including during prayer or in state of Ihram.

hanbali.dalil: Explicit inclusion in the five Fawasiq.

maliki.exceptions: If far away in its natural habitat and posing no threat, leaving it is preferred.

hanafi.exceptions: For domestic (house) snakes, one should warn them for three days first (unless aggressive), as they may be Jinn. If seen after three days, kill.

shafii.position: Permissible and recommended to kill immediately
shafii.exceptions: Domestic snakes in Madinah must be warned, according to some Shafi'i jurists, but venomous ones outside may be killed immediately.

hanbali.exceptions: Warning domestic snakes is recommended except for the short-tailed snake (al-Abtar) and the two-striped snake (Dhu al-Tufyatayn), which are killed instantly.

maliki.exceptions: Agree with warning house snakes, but permit immediate killing if danger is imminent.

hanafi.position: Prohibited to kill unless causing damage
hanafi.dalil: Hadith forbidding killing the ant (Abu Dawud 5267).
hanafi.exceptions: If ants invade the home, bite people, or damage food/property, they become classified as harmful (mu'dhi) and may be eliminated, but never by burning.

shafii.exceptions: Permissible to kill when they cause actual harm (e.g. carpenter ants destroying wood structures), using non-fire methods.

hanbali.exceptions: Permissible to repel and kill if they cause bodily or financial harm.

maliki.qiyas_basis: Avoidance of unnecessary destruction of life.
maliki.exceptions: Permissible to eliminate if they cause harm or contaminate living spaces. Burning remains strictly forbidden.
